extension.valueMarkdown:
Mid-30s Latina woman. Lived in Austin, TX through 2022, then moved to Oakland, CA.

Texas chapter: Reproductive history managed by an OB/GYN practice in Austin. First pregnancy (2019) was uncomplicated — vaginal delivery, healthy baby. In early 2020 she had a spontaneous abortion (miscarriage) at ~10 weeks — managed expectantly, emotionally difficult but no surgical intervention needed. Second successful pregnancy (2021) had mild gestational diabetes (diet-controlled, resolved postpartum), vaginal delivery. Also saw a PCP in Austin for routine care — annual wellness exams, immunizations, a couple of URIs. The miscarriage is particularly sensitive data — in a post-Dobbs environment, records of pregnancy loss could be misinterpreted or weaponized, making the case for granular data sharing controls viscerally concrete.

California chapter: Established with a new PCP in Oakland in late 2022. In early 2023, started having bilateral hand and wrist pain, morning stiffness lasting over an hour. PCP ordered initial labs (RF, anti-CCP, ESR, CRP — all elevated). Referred to rheumatology. Diagnosed with rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive. Started methotrexate 15mg weekly + folic acid. Required regular monitoring (CBC, LFTs every 8-12 weeks). Partial response to methotrexate — added adalimumab (Humira) in late 2023. Good response by 2024. Ongoing monitoring, quarterly rheumatology visits, regular labs.

reasonCode.text: Missed abortion diagnosed, expectant management counseling

resource.conclusion: Transvaginal ultrasound demonstrates intrauterine pregnancy with crown-rump length consistent with 9 weeks 4 days gestation. No fetal cardiac activity detected, confirming fetal demise (missed abortion). No adnexal mass or free fluid identified. Findings rule out ectopic pregnancy and hemorrhagic complication; patient is clinically stable.

note.text: First trimester intrauterine pregnancy measuring 9 weeks 4 days by crown-rump length on transvaginal ultrasound with no fetal cardiac activity. Patient is clinically stable with light spotting only. No adnexal mass or free fluid. Expectant management chosen after full counseling.

note.text: Diagnosed at 28 weeks gestation in third pregnancy. Abnormal 100 g 3-hour glucose tolerance test with 3 of 4 values abnormal. A1c 5.3% excludes pregestational diabetes. Initial management with diet, exercise, and home glucose monitoring.

Resolved postpartum. Six-week postpartum follow-up on 2022-06-10 confirmed normal fasting glucose (88 mg/dL) and A1c (5.4%), consistent with complete metabolic normalization after delivery. Patient counseled on elevated lifetime type 2 diabetes risk and recommended annual screening.
